sentiment_score_definition: x <= -0.35: Bearish; -0.35 < x <= -0.15: Somewhat-Bearish; -0.15 < x < 0.15: Neutral; 0.15 <= x < 0.35: Somewhat_Bullish; x >= 0.35: Bullish
relevance_score_definition: 0 < x <= 1, with a higher score indicating higher relevance.

feed.title: SM Energy to sell some Texas assets for $950 million
feed.summary: SM Energy Company announced the sale of some of its South Texas assets to Caturus for $950 million. The company, an independent energy producer, focuses on oil, gas, and natural gas liquid production primarily in the Midland Basin of West Texas and the Maverick Basin of South Texas. This sale reflects SM Energy's ongoing portfolio management and strategic adjustments within the energy sector.

feed.title: Philips Settles Suit Over Sleep Machine Starting Home Fire
feed.summary: Medical equipment company Philips has reached a settlement in a North Carolina federal court with a woman who sued them over a sleep apnea breathing machine. The woman alleged that the device caught fire, causing burns to her mother's face and destroying her home. The article mentions the date of record for the specific article is February 17, 2026, 1:41 PM EST.

feed.title: Federal Realty’s Retail Formula: Steady Growth, Durable Dividends
feed.summary: Federal Realty Investment Trust (FRT) focuses on open-air shopping centers and mixed-use developments in affluent markets, demonstrating steady growth and a commitment to its dividend. The company achieved record leasing volume in 2025 and maintains high occupancy, with a strong balance sheet supported by investment-grade ratings despite elevated leverage. FRT is notable for its "Dividend King" status, having consistently increased its dividend for decades, making it an attractive option for income investors seeking resilience and durability.

feed.title: Prudential Issues New Shares Under 2023 Employee Sharesave Plan
feed.summary: Prudential plc has issued 1,357 new ordinary shares under its 2023 Prudential Sharesave Plan, utilizing an existing block admission to the London Stock Exchange. This move aims to support employee ownership and incentives across its Asian and African growth markets with minimal dilution to existing shareholders. Analysts currently rate Prudential's stock as a Buy with a price target of £13.20.

feed.title: Former NetApp CTO wins dismissal of lawsuit after Vast Data deal
feed.summary: A Florida judge has dismissed NetApp's lawsuit against its former CTO, Jonsi Stefánsson, ruling that the case should be litigated in Iceland due to a mandatory forum selection clause in his employment contract. NetApp had accused Stefánsson of breach of contract and stealing trade secrets after he left the company to form Red Stapler, which was subsequently acquired by Vast Data. The court found that Stefánsson's employment agreement and a proprietary information agreement needed to be read together, with the Icelandic jurisdiction clause taking precedence.

feed.title: Goldman CEO Solomon, Long a Crypto Skeptic, Says He Owns Bitcoin
feed.summary: Goldman Sachs CEO David Solomon, previously a cryptocurrency skeptic, revealed he now owns a "very, very limited" amount of Bitcoin. He made this disclosure at the World Liberty Forum at Mar-a-Lago in Florida on Wednesday. This marks a shift in his public stance on cryptocurrencies.

feed.title: Hormel to Sell Whole-Bird Turkey Business
feed.summary: Hormel Foods Corp. has announced it will sell its whole-bird turkey business to Life-Science Innovations to reduce exposure to commodity market volatility. The sale includes a plant, feed mill, and transportation assets, but Hormel retains its Jennie-O brand. This move aligns with Hormel's strategy to focus on value-added proteins, especially in light of recent avian influenza outbreaks.

feed.title: Jefferies Adjusts Price Target on Textron to $115 From $110, Maintains Buy Rating
feed.summary: Jefferies has increased its price target for Textron (TXT) to $115 from the previous $110, while reiterating a Buy rating on the stock. This adjustment reflects a positive outlook from the analyst firm on the diversified industrial group, which specializes in aircraft, helicopters, industrial equipment, and defense systems. This news follows several other recent analyst adjustments and company announcements, including an equity buyback.

feed.title: MasTec Schedules Fourth Quarter and Full Year 2025 Earnings Conference Call
feed.summary: MasTec, Inc. (NYSE: MTZ) announced it will release its fourth quarter and full year financial results for 2025 on Thursday, February 26, 2026, after market close. Senior management will host a webcast the following day, Friday, February 27, 2026, at 9:00 a.m. ET, to discuss these results. The webcast will be available on MasTec's Investor Relations website.

feed.title: Booking Holdings Links Route 66 Centennial Deal To Valuation Story
feed.summary: Booking Holdings, through Booking.com, has become the Official Travel Partner for the Route 66 Centennial, set for 2026. This partnership is seen as a strategic move to boost brand visibility, particularly in domestic and road-trip segments, and could inform future product development in travel planning tools and cross-selling. While primarily a branding and customer acquisition play, investors should monitor its impact on US booking volumes and user engagement, especially given Booking Holdings' identified risk of high debt.

feed.title: Qualcomm's Analysts Are Throwing in the Towel-Time to Be Brave?
feed.summary: Shares of Qualcomm have significantly declined, prompting analysts to grow cautious, with some downgrading the stock amid concerns about its growth trajectory and exposure to the smartphone cycle. However, technical indicators suggest the stock is oversold, and some analysts maintain a bullish outlook, presenting a potential contrarian buying opportunity for investors willing to take a risk. The $135 level is identified as key support, with its ability to hold determining the stock's near-term direction.

feed.title: CoStar: U.S. retail vacancy expected to rise ‘minimally’ in first half of 2026
feed.summary: CoStar forecasts that U.S. retail vacancy will rise minimally in the first half of 2026 before declining slightly later in the year and into 2027. Despite receding, store closures are anticipated to increase in early 2026 as a bifurcated retail sales environment prompts some tenants to reduce locations. The stable outlook is supported by a resumption of positive demand in late 2025, although downside risks related to tariffs and potential price increases remain.

feed.title: WEC Energy Group SVP Straka sells $325k in stock
feed.summary: Mary Beth Straka, Senior VP at WEC Energy Group (NASDAQ:WEC), sold 2,815 shares of company stock for over $325,000 after exercising stock options. This transaction follows WEC Energy Group's recent financial results, which included an EPS miss but strong revenue exceeding forecasts and upgraded price targets from analysts. The company is noted for 56 consecutive years of dividend payments and a current dividend yield of 3.3%.

feed.title: Form 424B2 GOLDMAN SACHS GROUP INC
feed.summary: Goldman Sachs Group Inc. has filed Form 424B2 for $205,000,000 Floating Rate Notes due 2029. These notes will pay interest at a rate of compounded SOFR plus 0.86% per annum, with a minimum interest rate of 0.00%, payable quarterly. The filing details the specific terms, risk factors related to the structure, valuation, and market sales, as well as U.S. federal income tax consequences for investors.

feed.title: Apa stock hits 52-week high at $28.56 By Investing.com
feed.summary: APA Corporation's stock reached a new 52-week high at $28.56, reflecting a strong performance with a 36.74% return over the past six months and a 20.52% increase over the last year. The company maintains a "GREAT" financial health score and is considered undervalued, with analysts reiterating positive ratings and price targets. Upcoming earnings and strategic developments, including the sale of the Allgas network, are expected to influence future performance.

feed.title: SGH, Steel Dynamics bid $10.6B for BlueScope in ‘best and final’ offer
feed.summary: SGH Ltd and Steel Dynamics have increased their takeover offer for Australian steelmaker BlueScope Steel to A$15 billion ($10.62 billion), or A$32.35 per share, calling it a "best and final" bid. This revised offer is 8% higher than their previous bid and represents a 15.5% premium to BlueScope's last closing value. The acquiring companies plan to break up BlueScope, with SGH taking the Australian operations and Steel Dynamics the North American unit.

feed.title: ADP Just Raised Its Dividend 10% for the 50th Year in a Row
feed.summary: Automatic Data Processing (NASDAQ:ADP) has increased its quarterly dividend by 10% to $1.70 per share, marking its 50th consecutive year of dividend increases, qualifying it as a Dividend King. This consistent growth, coupled with a recession-resistant business model and a healthy free cash flow payout ratio of 50.3%, positions ADP as a reliable option for income-focused investors and retirees seeking steady dividend income with purchasing power protection. The company's annual dividend has grown by over 2,100% since 1999, demonstrating strong long-term compounding.

feed.title: Tesla racks up 14 Robotaxi crashes in Austin since launching last year
feed.summary: Tesla's Robotaxi service has been involved in 14 crashes in Austin since its June launch, with five occurring in December and January when the company began testing rides without safety monitors. While Tesla redacts crash details, all recent incidents involved property damage with no injuries. Competitor Waymo, with a significantly larger fleet, reported a similar number of crashes during the same period, though one Waymo incident involved minor injuries.

feed.title: Prudential Financial (NYSE:PRU) Director Thomas Stoddard Purchases 250 Shares
feed.summary: Prudential Financial Director Thomas Stoddard recently purchased 250 shares of the company's stock for approximately $25,742.50. This insider transaction comes as Prudential increased its quarterly dividend to $1.40, offering a 5.3% yield. Despite missing Q4 EPS estimates, the company's revenue beat expectations, and analysts generally hold a "Hold" rating with a consensus price target of $117.11.

feed.title: 50+ cocktail and food events across four Las Vegas resorts September weekend
feed.summary: MGM Resorts International is launching "DRINK Las Vegas," a four-day beverage and culinary festival from September 24-27, 2026, across Bellagio, ARIA, The Cosmopolitan, and Park MGM. The event will feature over 50 events including tastings, master classes, and chef-mixologist collaborations. This initiative aims to celebrate innovators in the beverage world and elevate cocktail culture through educational sessions and unique experiences.

feed.title: Caesars Entertainment Launches First Proprietary Online Slot, Ca$hline™
feed.summary: Caesars Entertainment has launched Ca$hline, its first proprietary online slot game developed by its in-house studio, Empire Creative™. This new three-reel stepper slot offers players enhanced control and engagement through its "Land It, Win It" format and features like a 2x Multiplier, Reverse Respin, and Wheel Bonus. Ca$hline is currently available in New Jersey, with plans for expansion, and integrates with the Caesars Rewards loyalty program, reinforcing Caesars' commitment to original digital content and responsible gaming.

feed.title: Medtronic nets FDA clearance for robotic spine system
feed.summary: Medtronic has received FDA clearance for its new Stealth AXiS surgical system, a next-generation robotic platform for spine surgery. This system integrates AI-based planning, real-time navigation, and robotic assistance, building on Medtronic's acquisition of Mazor Robotics. It is designed for use in both hospitals and ambulatory surgical centers and aims to increase Medtronic's market share in the cranial and spinal technologies sector.

feed.title: Advisor moves: Osaic reels in more than $440M with Ameriprise additions
feed.summary: Osaic has successfully recruited two teams from Ameriprise, adding over $440 million in client assets through existing OSJs in Phoenix and Westport. Concurrently, Wells Fargo Advisors expanded its Private Client Group by bringing in a $480 million team from UBS in Alabama, while Cetera welcomed a $205 million team from Wells Fargo's independent channel. These moves highlight significant recruitment activity across major financial advisory firms.

feed.title: BLK.SW BlackRock (SIX) +96.87% to CHF852.85 18 Feb after hours: volume alert
feed.summary: BlackRock (BLK.SW) stock saw a dramatic 96.87% jump to CHF852.85 in after-hours trading on February 18, 2026, though this significant movement involved only 40 reported shares, prompting a volume alert. The article discusses the reasons behind this unusual re-pricing, including sector flows and potential catalysts, while also providing a detailed valuation, technical analysis, and risk factors for the stock. Meyka AI gives BLK.SW a "BUY" grade with a score of 74.14, though its model forecasts project a modest downside from the current price over the next 1-5 years.

feed.title: Using behavioral science to unlock AI for industrial sectors
authors: Katie Morton and Ho Chee (Angie) Choi
feed.summary: This article discusses how behavioral science can be used to improve AI adoption and responsible use in industrial sectors. It highlights that successful AI implementation is not just a technical challenge but a behavioral one, requiring organizations to address challenges related to human capability, opportunity, and motivation. The authors propose integrating behavior change techniques into AI workflows, managing technostress, and designing AI for autonomous operations that keep human involvement visible and supported.

feed.title: NEA: Looking At The Positives, And A Future Concern
feed.summary: The Nuveen AMT-Free Quality Municipal Income Fund (NEA) experienced a significant Q4 rally and increased income, benefiting investors. While this boost is positive for short-term holders, its sustainability is questionable, prompting caution for long-term investors. A favorable macroeconomic environment with lower inflation suggests that the Federal Reserve is unlikely to raise interest rates further, which is generally positive for bonds, including municipal bonds like those held by NEA.

feed.title: News | Fast-tracking the fighter jet engine of the future
feed.summary: Pratt & Whitney, an RTX business, is using digital tools to rapidly develop the XA103 adaptive fighter jet engine for the U.S. Air Force, aiming for air dominance. This fully digital design approach, including a collaborative environment and model-based systems, reduces errors, shortens development times, and enhances communication with suppliers and the Air Force. The company expects these innovations to deliver next-generation capabilities faster, building on past successes like the F119 engine.

feed.title: Arm shares edge higher as Nvidia sells entire stake in company
feed.summary: Nvidia has sold its entire stake of 1.1 million shares in British semiconductor firm Arm, valued at $155.8 million, as revealed in a recent SEC filing. Despite this divestment, Arm's New York-listed shares rose over 1%, and the two companies maintain a strong relationship through a 20-year licensing agreement. Nvidia's exit follows its failed 2022 bid to acquire Arm for $40 billion, and Arm recently reported better-than-expected fiscal third-quarter sales, driven by AI project momentum.

feed.title: Analog Devices: Fiscal Q1 Earnings Snapshot
feed.summary: Analog Devices Inc. (ADI) reported a strong fiscal first quarter, with profits of $830.8 million and adjusted earnings of $2.46 per share, exceeding Wall Street expectations. The semiconductor maker also surpassed revenue forecasts, bringing in $3.16 billion, and provided optimistic guidance for the fiscal second quarter. ADI shares have performed well, rising 24% year-to-date and 57% over the last 12 months.

feed.title: Analog Devices reports strong Q4 earnings driven by industrial and communications growth
feed.summary: Analog Devices Inc. reported Q4 fiscal year 2025 earnings that surpassed Wall Street expectations, with revenue of $3.16 billion and adjusted EPS of $2.46, driven by strong growth in its Industrial and Communications markets. The company also announced an 11% dividend increase and provided optimistic guidance for Q1 fiscal year 2026, forecasting higher revenue and EPS than analyst estimates. Shares rose approximately 3% in after-hours trading following the announcement.

feed.title: From Rides To Robots: Uber’s Path Through The AV And AI Era (NYSE:UBER)
feed.summary: Uber Technologies delivered strong 2024 results, with revenue up 18% to $52B and normalized EPS rising 82% to $2.46/share. The company's hybrid model, leveraging both autonomous vehicles (AVs) and human drivers, and the Uber One loyalty program, along with accelerating advertising revenues, are enhancing monetization and operational resilience. However, long-term risks include potential disintermediation from AVs and agentic AI, which could impact Uber's aggregator advantage.

feed.title: eBay Earnings on Deck – Stock Higher Into The Print
feed.summary: eBay (EBAY) is set to report its fourth-quarter 2025 earnings after market close on February 18, 2026, with analysts expecting $1.33 EPS and $2.73 billion in revenue. The stock, currently at $82.33, is up 2.98% on the day but down 19% from its 52-week high, reflecting investor focus on whether its platform transformation strategy can drive sustainable advertising revenue growth and improve margins. The primary driver for post-earnings stock movement will be management's guidance for 2026, particularly regarding advertising revenue trajectory and margin expectations.

feed.title: After a 10% Decline Last Year, Is Philips Stock a Still Buy in 2026?
feed.summary: Philips N.V. stock recently jumped 10% after Q4 results reinforced a positive margin recovery narrative, with a guided 12.5% adjusted EBITA margin for 2026 despite tariff impacts. The company also secured a €23.5 million grant for brain treatment research. However, a valuation model suggests a "Sell" recommendation, projecting only an 8% total upside over three years due to risks including tariff exposure and cautious China assumptions, leading to an annualized return below the 10% equity hurdle rate.

feed.title: Fitness Wearable Boom Drives Record Profits For Garmin
feed.summary: Garmin reported record-breaking fourth-quarter 2025 results, with significant revenue growth in its fitness segment, driven by strong demand for advanced wearables. The company surpassed analyst estimates for both revenue and adjusted EPS, and announced plans to increase its quarterly dividend and initiate a new share repurchase program. Garmin also provided a strong outlook for 2026, expecting higher revenue and adjusted EPS than current analyst consensus.

feed.title: BofA Securities Cuts Price Target on Cadence Design Systems to $375 From $400, Maintains Buy Rating
feed.summary: BofA Securities has lowered its price target for Cadence Design Systems (CDNS) to $375 from $400, while continuing to recommend a "Buy" rating for the stock. This adjustment comes despite Cadence Design Systems reporting an earnings beat in Q4 and being viewed as a beneficiary of AI demand. Other analysts like Piper Sandler, Morgan Stanley, Berenberg Bank, and Loop Capital also adjusted their price targets for Cadence, with varying rating changes.

feed.title: Microsoft vs. Oracle: Which Cloud & AI Giant Has an Edge Right Now?
feed.summary: This article compares Microsoft (MSFT) and Oracle (ORCL) as leading cloud and AI companies, analyzing their financial performance, AI strategies, and market valuations. It concludes that Microsoft holds a stronger position due to its scale, profitability, diverse AI monetization, and robust balance sheet, despite Oracle's significant investments in OCI and a growing backlog. The article advises investors to look for an entry point in Microsoft and to hold or cautiously approach Oracle.

feed.title: RLJ Lodging Trust Completes Refinancing Transactions
feed.summary: RLJ Lodging Trust announced the successful refinancing of all its debt maturities through 2028, strengthening its balance sheet and extending its debt maturity schedule. The refinancing includes extending its revolver, upsizing and recasting an existing term loan, and adding a new seven-year term loan. This strategic move allows the company to address its $500 million senior notes maturing in July 2026, with no further debt maturities until 2029.

feed.title: Horizon Kinetics buys Texas Pacific Land (TPL) share for $425
feed.summary: Horizon Kinetics Asset Management LLC, a significant owner of Texas Pacific Land Corp (TPL), recently purchased 1 share of TPL common stock for $425.55. Following this transaction, Horizon Kinetics directly holds over 3.4 million shares. The stock is currently trading slightly above the purchase price, and InvestingPro data suggests it may be in overbought territory.

feed.title: From gym floor to global stage: Built For Athletes powers up with NatWest’s £1m growth investment
feed.summary: NatWest has provided Warrington-based Built For Athletes with a £1.025 million funding package to support its global expansion and sustainability goals. The company, founded in 2018 by Nicholas Costello, specializes in design-protected backpacks for fitness enthusiasts and has partnered with global brands like Alpine F1 and Borussia Dortmund FC. The investment, comprising a trade loan and invoice finance, will enable product development, operational capability, and international growth.

feed.title: Eli Lilly and Company (LLY) Stock: Dips After New Psoriasis-Obesity Trial Data
feed.summary: Eli Lilly's stock saw a 1.65% decline to $1,018.92 following the announcement of positive Phase 3b TOGETHER-PsO trial results. The trial demonstrated that a combination of Taltz and Zepbound significantly improved skin clearance and weight loss in patients with psoriasis and obesity compared to Taltz alone. Despite the clinical success, which offers a comprehensive treatment for these comorbid conditions, the market reacted with a short-term dip in the company's share price.

feed.title: Pfizer Targets Real-World Myeloma Data in Taiwan With New Elranatamab Study
feed.summary: Pfizer Inc. is initiating a new real-world observational study in Taiwan to evaluate the effectiveness of its multiple myeloma drug, elranatamab, in a routine hospital environment. This study aims to collect data on actual patient outcomes and treatment patterns, supporting the drug's longer-term acceptance and market share in Asia. While the study is not yet recruiting, its progress indicates Pfizer's commitment to its oncology portfolio, with potential positive impacts on pricing and reimbursement discussions in the future.

feed.title: Takeda’s HyQvia Study Tracks Real-World Use in Multiple Myeloma, Offering Gradual Upside for TAK Investors
feed.summary: Takeda Pharmaceutical Company (TAK) is conducting a prospective, observational study called "A Prospective, Observational Study to Assess the Real-World Usage and Outcomes of HyQvia in Patients With Multiple Myeloma (MM) Diagnosed With Secondary Immunodeficiency (SID)." The study aims to track the real-world use of HyQvia in adults with multiple myeloma and secondary immune weakness to assess its safety and outcomes. This ongoing study could provide modest medium-term upside for TAK investors by reinforcing revenue durability and supporting valuation multiples in the specialty plasma and rare disease segment through positive real-world evidence.

feed.title: Rail News - CSX trained thousands of first responders in 2025. For Railroad Career Professionals
feed.summary: CSX trained 5,645 first responders in 2025 through 75 incident training sessions focused on railroad-related emergencies. A significant enhancement to their Responder Incident Training (RIT) program was the introduction of a dedicated training locomotive, allowing for realistic, hands-on learning experiences. The company aims to prepare first responders to protect communities, employees, and the rail network during emergencies.

feed.title: Colgate-Palmolive’s Pension Class Deal Nabs Lawyers $96 Million
feed.summary: Lawyers representing retirees of Colgate-Palmolive Co. have been awarded over $96 million in attorneys' fees from a $332 million class settlement. This settlement addresses allegations that the consumer product maker shortchanged pension benefits. A federal judge deemed the 29% fee reasonable given the case's complexity and duration, also approving $2.9 million in litigation expenses and a $10,000 service award for the lead plaintiff.

feed.title: Technology at the service of the longevity revolution
feed.summary: Spain faces the significant challenge of an aging population and increasing dependency costs, which could overwhelm the social protection system. The article proposes leveraging technological innovation to transform dependency into autonomy for older adults, focusing on training and inclusive design to mitigate the digital divide. By using solutions like AI-powered monitoring, predictive telecare, and domestic robots, technology can help improve care, reduce the burden on caregivers, and ensure a better quality of life for the elderly.

feed.title: Payments Power Play: MA or AXP, Who Has the Deeper Moat in 2026?
feed.summary: This article analyzes the competitive advantages of Mastercard (MA) and American Express (AXP) in the global payments landscape, determining which company has a "deeper moat" for 2026. Mastercard's asset-light network model, strong margins, and diversified ecosystem are highlighted as key strengths, while American Express benefits from its closed-loop model and premium customer base but faces higher credit risk. Although both are high-quality companies, Mastercard is assessed as better positioned for durable upside due to its stability and diversified growth drivers.

feed.title: Payments Power Play: MA or AXP, Who Has the Deeper Moat in 2026?
feed.summary: This article analyzes Mastercard (MA) and American Express (AXP) to determine which company possesses a deeper competitive moat in 2026. Mastercard's asset-light network model and global diversification are highlighted as key strengths, contrasting with American Express's closed-loop model and direct lending exposure. While both companies are strong contenders in the digital payments landscape, Mastercard is considered better positioned for durable upside due to its stability, lower risk profile, and robust value-added services.

feed.title: Trimble sponsors engineering student competition
feed.summary: Trimble has announced a two-year sponsorship for the Engineering for People Design Challenge, an initiative led by Engineers Without Borders UK and South Africa. This competition tasks university students with developing sustainable engineering solutions for underserved communities, with the 2025/2026 challenge focusing on Ladywood in Birmingham, England. Trimble's expanded support builds on its history of contributions to Engineers Without Borders, aiming to foster critical skills in ethical decision-making, cultural sensitivity, and community engagement among future engineers.

feed.title: NCLH’s Sommer ‘excited’ for retirement, cruise expert weighs in
feed.summary: Harry Sommer, former President and CEO of Norwegian Cruise Line Holdings Ltd (NCLH), has retired "suddenly" after the company's board appointed John W. Chidsey as his successor with immediate effect. Sommer expressed excitement for his retirement on LinkedIn, focusing on family and personal passions, while luxury cruise veteran Larry Pimentel noted that such immediate exits often stem from performance-based reasons, with the board losing confidence in leadership's ability to create shareholder value. Pimentel interpreted the board's language as indicating a need for a "turnaround specialist" to "sharpen execution, improve performance, strengthen financial performance, reduce leverage."

feed.title: USA Compression Partners, LP (NYSE:USAC) Receives Average Recommendation of "Reduce" from Analysts
feed.summary: USA Compression Partners, LP (NYSE:USAC) has received an average "Reduce" recommendation from analysts, with five brokerages covering the stock, including one sell and four hold ratings. The average one-year price target is $26.75. Despite reporting in-line Q4 EPS of $0.28 and record Q4 revenue of $252.5M, the company has a high dividend payout ratio of 262.5% and a market cap of $3.25 billion.
